'''Katori Shrine''' (香取神社, Katori Jinja) refers to Shinto shrines named "Katori" dedicated to the deity {{ill|Futsunushi|en|Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi}}.
There are approximately 400 shrines with this name across Japan, centered primarily around the {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region}}, with the {{ill|Katori Shrine|en|Katori Shrine|WD=Q372380|lt=Katori Jingū}} in {{ill|Katori, Chiba|en|Katori, Chiba|WD=Q858357|lt=Katori, Chiba}} as their head shrine. Most Katori Shrines were founded as branch shrines of Katori Jingū, sharing the same enshrined deity, Futsunushi.
Their distribution is mainly along the {{ill|Tone River|en|Tone River|WD=Q177452|lt=Tone River}} and {{ill|Edo River|en|Edo River|WD=Q1194505|lt=Edo River}}. Along the southern {{ill|Arakawa River (Kantō)|en|Arakawa River (Kantō)|WD=Q625465|lt=Arakawa River (Kantō)}} lie {{ill|Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)|en|Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)|WD=Q703633|lt=Hikawa Shrines}}, and along the {{ill|Moto-Arakawa River|ja|元荒川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Arakawa River|1=Moto-Arakawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11388047}} between them lie {{ill|Hisaizu Shrines|ja|久伊豆神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisaizu Shrines|1=Hisaizu-jinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55523154}}. According to {{ill|Nishitsunoi Masayoshi|ja|西角井正慶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitsunoi Masayoshi|qq=}}, the distribution areas of these shrines are distinct without overlap. The distribution area of Katori Shrines covers former marshland reclaimed from the 10th century onward.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:香取神社]][[zh:香取神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:香取神社]][[zh:香取神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:香取神社]][[zh:香取神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:香取神社]][[zh:香取神社]]
'''Katori Shrine''' (香取神社, Katori Jinja) refers to Shinto shrines named "Katori" dedicated to the deity {{ill|Futsunushi|en|Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi}}.
There are approximately 400 shrines with this name across Japan, centered primarily around the {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region}}, with the {{ill|Katori Shrine|en|Katori Shrine|WD=Q372380|lt=Katori Jingū}} in {{ill|Katori, Chiba|en|Katori, Chiba|WD=Q858357|lt=Katori, Chiba}} as their head shrine. Most Katori Shrines were founded as branch shrines of Katori Jingū, sharing the same enshrined deity, Futsunushi.
Their distribution is mainly along the {{ill|Tone River|en|Tone River|WD=Q177452|lt=Tone River}} and {{ill|Edo River|en|Edo River|WD=Q1194505|lt=Edo River}}. Along the southern {{ill|Arakawa River (Kantō)|en|Arakawa River (Kantō)|WD=Q625465|lt=Arakawa River (Kantō)}} lie {{ill|Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)|en|Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)|WD=Q703633|lt=Hikawa Shrines}}, and along the {{ill|Moto-Arakawa River|ja|元荒川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Arakawa River|1=Moto-Arakawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11388047}} between them lie {{ill|Hisaizu Shrines|ja|久伊豆神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisaizu Shrines|1=Hisaizu-jinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55523154}}. According to {{ill|Nishitsunoi Masayoshi|ja|西角井正慶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitsunoi Masayoshi|qq=}}, the distribution areas of these shrines are distinct without overlap. The distribution area of Katori Shrines covers former marshland reclaimed from the 10th century onward.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:香取神社]][[zh:香取神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:香取神社]][[zh:香取神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:香取神社]][[zh:香取神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:香取神社]][[zh:香取神社]]
'''Katori Shrine''' (香取神社, Katori Jinja) refers to Shinto shrines named "Katori" dedicated to the deity {{ill|Futsunushi|en|Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|1=Futsunushi}}.
There are approximately 400 shrines with this name across Japan, centered primarily around the {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}}, with the {{ill|Katori Shrine|en|Katori Shrine|WD=Q372380|lt=Katori Jingū|1=Katori Jingū}} in {{ill|Katori, Chiba|en|Katori, Chiba|WD=Q858357|lt=Katori, Chiba|1=Katori, Chiba}} as their head shrine. Most Katori Shrines were founded as branch shrines of Katori Jingū, sharing the same enshrined deity, Futsunushi.
Their distribution is mainly along the {{ill|Tone River|en|Tone River|WD=Q177452|lt=Tone River|1=Tone River}} and {{ill|Edo River|en|Edo River|WD=Q1194505|lt=Edo River|1=Edo River}}. Along the southern {{ill|Arakawa River (Kantō)|en|Arakawa River (Kantō)|WD=Q625465|lt=Arakawa River (Kantō)|1=Arakawa River (Kantō)}} lie {{ill|Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)|en|Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)|WD=Q703633|lt=Hikawa Shrines|1=Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)}}, and along the {{ill|Moto-Arakawa River|ja|元荒川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Arakawa River|1=Moto-Arakawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11388047}} between them lie {{ill|Hisaizu Shrines|ja|久伊豆神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisaizu Shrines|1=Hisaizu-jinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55523154}}. According to {{ill|Nishitsunoi Masayoshi|ja|西角井正慶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitsunoi Masayoshi|qq=}}, the distribution areas of these shrines are distinct without overlap. The distribution area of Katori Shrines covers former marshland reclaimed from the 10th century onward.
==Main Katori Shrines==
===Hokkaidō and Tōhoku Region=== * {{ill|Kashima Katori Shrine (Chitose)|ja|鹿島香取神社 (千歳市)|lt=Kashima Katori Shrine|lt_ja=鹿島香取神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Katori Shrine (Chitose)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506092|1=Kashima Katori Shrine (Chitose)}} (Chitose, Hokkaidō) * {{ill|Katori Shrine (Aomori)|ja|香取神社 (青森市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori Shrine (Aomori)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579691|1=Katori Shrine (Aomori)}} (Aomori, Aomori) * {{ill|Katori Shrine (Hirosaki)|ja|香取神社 (弘前市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori Shrine (Hirosaki)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579692|1=Katori Shrine (Hirosaki)}} (Hirosaki, Aomori) * {{ill|Kashima Katori Shrine (Aoba, Sendai)|ja|鹿島香取神社 (仙台市青葉区)|lt=Kashima Katori Shrine|lt_ja=鹿島香取神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Katori Shrine (Aoba, Sendai)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506094|1=Kashima Katori Shrine (Aoba, Sendai)}} (Aoba-ku, Sendai, Miyagi) * {{ill|Katori Izunomiko Shrine|ja|香取伊豆乃御子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori Izunomiko Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579693|1=Katori Izunomiko Shrine}} (Ishinomaki, Miyagi) * {{ill|Katori Shrine (Kakuda)|ja|香取神社 (角田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori Shrine (Kakuda)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579694|1=Katori Shrine (Kakuda)}} (Kakuda, Miyagi) * {{ill|Katori Shrine (Nikaho)|ja|香取神社 (にかほ市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori Shrine (Nikaho)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579695|1=Katori Shrine (Nikaho)}} (Nikaho, Akita) * {{ill|Katori Shrine (Sukagawa)|ja|香取神社 (須賀川市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori Shrine (Sukagawa)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579696|1=Katori Shrine (Sukagawa)}} (Sukagawa, Fukushima) * {{ill|Katori Shrine (Minamiaizu)|ja|香取神社 (南会津町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori Shrine (Minamiaizu)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579697|1=Katori Shrine (Minamiaizu)}} (Minamiaizu, Fukushima)

[[ja:香取神社]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh:香取神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11667187}} [[Category:Katori Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines by tradition]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:香取神社]][[zh:香取神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:香取神社]][[zh:香取神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:香取神社]][[zh:香取神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:香取神社]][[zh:香取神社]]
'''Katori Shrine''' (香取神社, Katori Jinja) refers to Shinto shrines named "Katori" dedicated to the deity {{ill|Futsunushi|en|Futsunushi|qid=Q1107286|qid=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|1=Futsunushi}}.
There are approximately 400 shrines with this name across Japan, centered primarily around the {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|qid=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}}, with the {{ill|Katori Shrine|en|Katori Shrine|qid=Q372380|lt=Katori Jingū|1=Katori Jingū}} in {{ill|Katori, Chiba|en|Katori, Chiba|qid=Q858357|lt=Katori, Chiba|1=Katori, Chiba}} as their head shrine. Most Katori Shrines were founded as branch shrines of Katori Jingū, sharing the same enshrined deity, Futsunushi.
Their distribution is mainly along the {{ill|Tone River|en|Tone River|qid=Q177452|lt=Tone River|1=Tone River}} and {{ill|Edo River|en|Edo River|qid=Q1194505|lt=Edo River|1=Edo River}}. Along the southern {{ill|Arakawa River (Kantō)|en|Arakawa River (Kantō)|qid=Q625465|lt=Arakawa River (Kantō)|1=Arakawa River (Kantō)}} lie {{ill|Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)|en|Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)|qid=Q703633|lt=Hikawa Shrines|1=Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)}}, and along the {{ill|Moto-Arakawa River|ja|元荒川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Arakawa River|1=Moto-Arakawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11388047}} between them lie {{ill|Hisaizu Shrines|ja|久伊豆神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisaizu Shrines|1=Hisaizu-jinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q55523154}}. According to {{ill|Nishitsunoi Masayoshi|ja|西角井正慶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitsunoi Masayoshi|qq=}}, the distribution areas of these shrines are distinct without overlap. The distribution area of Katori Shrines covers former marshland reclaimed from the 10th century onward.
==Main Katori Shrines==
===Hokkaidō and Tōhoku Region=== * {{ill|Kashima Katori Shrine (Chitose)|ja|鹿島香取神社 (千歳市)|lt=Kashima Katori Shrine|lt_ja=鹿島香取神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Katori Shrine (Chitose)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506092|1=Kashima Katori Shrine (Chitose)}} (Chitose, Hokkaidō) * {{ill|Katori Shrine (Aomori)|ja|香取神社 (青森市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori Shrine (Aomori)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579691|1=Katori Shrine (Aomori)}} (Aomori, Aomori) * {{ill|Katori Shrine (Hirosaki)|ja|香取神社 (弘前市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori Shrine (Hirosaki)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579692|1=Katori Shrine (Hirosaki)}} (Hirosaki, Aomori) * {{ill|Kashima Katori Shrine (Aoba, Sendai)|ja|鹿島香取神社 (仙台市青葉区)|lt=Kashima Katori Shrine|lt_ja=鹿島香取神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Katori Shrine (Aoba, Sendai)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506094|1=Kashima Katori Shrine (Aoba, Sendai)}} (Aoba-ku, Sendai, Miyagi) * {{ill|Katori Izunomiko Shrine|ja|香取伊豆乃御子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori Izunomiko Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579693|1=Katori Izunomiko Shrine}} (Ishinomaki, Miyagi) * {{ill|Katori Shrine (Kakuda)|ja|香取神社 (角田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori Shrine (Kakuda)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579694|1=Katori Shrine (Kakuda)}} (Kakuda, Miyagi) * {{ill|Katori Shrine (Nikaho)|ja|香取神社 (にかほ市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori Shrine (Nikaho)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579695|1=Katori Shrine (Nikaho)}} (Nikaho, Akita) * {{ill|Katori Shrine (Sukagawa)|ja|香取神社 (須賀川市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori Shrine (Sukagawa)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579696|1=Katori Shrine (Sukagawa)}} (Sukagawa, Fukushima) * {{ill|Katori Shrine (Minamiaizu)|ja|香取神社 (南会津町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori Shrine (Minamiaizu)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579697|1=Katori Shrine (Minamiaizu)}} (Minamiaizu, Fukushima)
